sanskrit:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति॥
transliteration:
dehino 'smin yathā dehe kaumāraṃ yauvanaṃ jarā |
tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati ||
literal_meaning: Just as the embodied (soul) passes through boyhood, youth and old age in this body, so it passes into another body; the wise person is not bewildered by this.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s grief by pointing to the continuity of consciousness beneath bodily change. Psychologically, this is an invitation to shift identity from the transient body-ego to the observing Self, reducing death-anxiety through cognitive re-appraisal and existential reframing.
psychological_state: Arjuna is experiencing acute death-anxiety and identity diffusion; Krishna offers a stabilizing meta-cognitive perspective that dis-identifies from bodily changes.
trigger_scenario: A 45-year-old notices her first gray hairs and feels panic about 'losing herself'; instead of cosmetic denial she practices mindfulness of the unchanging witness within.
anchor_text: You are the unchanging witness within every changing body.
